description: Extend the unified query CLI playground with local file support (text/CSV/JSON), PPL grok/patterns validation, pre-built log format parsing, context-aware completion, inline bar charts, machine-readable JSON output for AI agent cooperation, and script mode. Building on the existing JLine3 REPL (US-001 to US-009) to validate features for the intern project: OpenSearch PPL/SQL Interactive Playground CLI.

userStories.title: Promote SimpleTable to api main source set
userStories.description: As a unified query API consumer, I want SimpleTable available as a public class in the api module's main source set so that I can create in-memory tables without depending on testFixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: SimpleTable.java exists at api/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/api/SimpleTable.java as a public class
SimpleTable has the same @Builder API: .col(name, SqlTypeName), .row(Object[]), .build()
SimpleTable implements ScannableTable with scan(), getRowType(), getStatistic(), getJdbcTableType()
UnifiedQueryTestBase in testFixtures imports and uses the promoted SimpleTable (no duplication)
Existing api module tests pass without modification
Build passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create cli Gradle module with dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a new cli/ Gradle module registered in the project so that CLI code has a proper build home with JLine3 and PicoCLI dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: settings.gradle includes 'cli'
cli/build.gradle exists with plugins: java-library, io.freefair.lombok, jacoco, com.diffplug.spotless, application
cli/build.gradle has mainClass set to org.opensearch.sql.cli.Main
cli/build.gradle depends on project(':api'), org.jline:jline:3.28.0, info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6
cli/build.gradle has spotless config matching api/build.gradle
A minimal Main.java exists at cli/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/cli/Main.java with a main method that prints 'OpenSearch Unified Query Shell' and exits
Build passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement SampleDataLoader
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to load sample data from a JSON file where each top-level key becomes a table so that I can query example data immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: SampleDataLoader.java exists at cli/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/cli/SampleDataLoader.java
SampleDataLoader.load(InputStream) returns Map<String, Table> where keys are table names
JSON format: top-level object with keys mapping to arrays of objects, each object is a row
Type inference from first row: JSON integer->INTEGER, float->DOUBLE, string->VARCHAR, boolean->BOOLEAN, null->VARCHAR
Each table is a SimpleTable instance with inferred schema and all rows
SampleDataLoader.loadFromClasspath(String resourcePath) loads from classpath resources
Default dataset hr.json exists at cli/src/main/resources/data/hr.json with employees (4+ rows) and departments (3+ rows) tables
Example dataset cli/examples/hr.json exists with same content
Unit test SampleDataLoaderTest verifies loading hr.json produces correct table names, column types, and row counts
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use Jackson for JSON parsing (already available transitively via Calcite). Nested objects/arrays: skip column and log warning.

userStories.title: Implement ResultSetFormatter
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want query results displayed as a formatted ASCII table so that I can read the output easily.
acceptanceCriteria: ResultSetFormatter.java exists at cli/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/cli/ResultSetFormatter.java
ResultSetFormatter.format(ResultSet, PrintStream) prints an ASCII table with column headers, separator lines, data rows, and row count
Column widths are computed from max of header length and data length
NULL values display as 'NULL'
Row count line shows 'N row(s) returned'
Unit test ResultSetFormatterTest verifies formatted output with mock ResultSet
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the iteration pattern from ResultSetAssertion.ResultSetVerifier in api testFixtures.

userStories.title: Implement QueryRepl core loop with meta-commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want an interactive REPL that accepts SQL/PPL queries and meta-commands so that I can explore data interactively.
acceptanceCriteria: QueryRepl.java exists at cli/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/cli/QueryRepl.java
QueryRepl creates UnifiedQueryContext, UnifiedQueryPlanner, UnifiedQueryCompiler from loaded tables and language
REPL loop reads input via JLine3 LineReader with persistent history file
Prompt shows current language: 'ppl> ' or 'sql> '

Meta-commands supported: .quit/.exit (exit), .help (print help), .language sql/.language ppl (switch language), .tables (list tables and columns), .schema <table> (show column details), .load <path> (load new JSON data file)
.language rebuilds context with new QueryType while retaining loaded tables
.load reads a new JSON file via SampleDataLoader and rebuilds context
Non-meta-command input is executed as query: plan() -> compile() -> executeQuery() -> ResultSetFormatter
Errors print a clean message (not stack trace): SyntaxCheckException shows syntax error, other exceptions show cause message
Ctrl+C interrupts current input, Ctrl+D exits REPL
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use JLine3 TerminalBuilder.builder().system(true).build() and LineReaderBuilder. History file at ~/.opensearch-query-history. Default catalog name: 'catalog'.

userStories.title: Implement Main entry point with PicoCLI
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want command-line flags to configure the CLI (data file, language, single query execution) so that I can use it in both interactive and non-interactive modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Main.java at cli/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/cli/Main.java uses PicoCLI @Command annotation
Supports --data/-d <path> flag to specify JSON data file (default: built-in hr.json from classpath)
Supports --language/-l <sql|ppl> flag to set initial language (default: ppl)
Supports --execute/-e <query> flag for non-interactive single query execution
Supports --help/-h flag for usage info
Non-interactive mode (-e): loads data, executes query, prints result, exits with 0 on success or 1 on error
Interactive mode (no -e): prints welcome banner with loaded table names, enters QueryRepl loop
Welcome banner shows: shell name, loaded dataset info (table names and row counts)
Build passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add tab completion for SQL/PPL keywords and table/column names
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want tab completion for keywords and table/column names so that I can type queries faster.
acceptanceCriteria: QueryCompleter.java exists at cli/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/cli/QueryCompleter.java
SQL mode: completes SQL keywords (SELECT, FROM, WHERE, GROUP, BY, ORDER, JOIN, HAVING, LIMIT, AS, ON, AND, OR, NOT, IN, BETWEEN, LIKE, IS, NULL, etc.)
PPL mode: completes PPL keywords (source, where, fields, stats, sort, eval, head, tail, top, rare, dedup, rename, parse, grok, patterns, lookup, join, etc.)
Both modes: completes loaded table names and column names
Completer is wired into JLine3 LineReader via LineReaderBuilder.completer()
Completer updates when language switches or new data is loaded
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use JLine3 AggregateCompleter with StringsCompleter for keywords and a custom Completer for table/column names from the loaded schema.

userStories.title: Add syntax highlighting for SQL/PPL
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want syntax highlighting in the REPL so that I can visually distinguish keywords, strings, and numbers while typing.
acceptanceCriteria: QueryHighlighter.java exists at cli/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/cli/QueryHighlighter.java implementing JLine3 Highlighter interface
SQL/PPL keywords are highlighted in bold or a distinct color
String literals (single-quoted) are highlighted in a different color
Numbers are highlighted
Meta-commands (. prefix) are highlighted
Highlighter is wired into JLine3 LineReader via LineReaderBuilder.highlighter()
Highlighter updates keyword set when language switches
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implement JLine3 Highlighter.highlight(LineReader, String) returning AttributedString. Use AttributedStringBuilder with AttributedStyle for coloring. Borrow keyword detection pattern from SQLLine's SqlLineHighlighter.

userStories.title: Add ecommerce example dataset and end-to-end verification
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want a richer example dataset for JOIN demos and I want confidence the full flow works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: cli/examples/ecommerce.json exists with orders (5+ rows), products (5+ rows), customers (3+ rows) tables
Non-interactive PPL works: ./gradlew :cli:run --args="-e 'source = catalog.employees | where age > 30'" prints correct ASCII table
Non-interactive SQL works: ./gradlew :cli:run --args="-l sql -e 'SELECT * FROM catalog.employees WHERE age > 30'" prints correct ASCII table
Custom data file works: ./gradlew :cli:run --args="-d cli/examples/ecommerce.json -l sql -e 'SELECT * FROM catalog.customers'" prints correct ASCII table
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the final verification story. Fix any integration issues discovered during end-to-end testing.

userStories.title: Load raw text files as line-per-row tables
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to .load a raw text file (like opensearch.log) so that each line becomes a row with a single 'line' column, enabling PPL analysis of semi-structured logs.
acceptanceCriteria: SampleDataLoader (or new TextFileLoader) detects file type by extension: .json uses existing JSON loading, .csv/.log/.txt uses line-per-row loading
.load <path> as <name> meta-command works in QueryRepl for text files
Each line of the text file becomes a row in a SimpleTable with one VARCHAR column named 'line'
Empty lines are preserved as empty string rows
Non-interactive mode works: -d opensearch.log -e 'source = catalog.logs | head 5' shows 5 log lines
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Foundation for all log analysis features. The .load meta-command should accept 'as <tablename>' syntax to name the table. File type detection: .json -> JSON loader, .csv -> CSV loader (future), everything else -> line-per-row.

userStories.title: Multi-line log joining for stack traces
userStories.description: As a CLI user loading opensearch.log, I want continuation lines (Java stack traces) joined to the previous log entry so that each row represents a complete log event.
acceptanceCriteria: When loading .log files, lines NOT starting with '[' are appended to the previous row's 'line' value with newline separator
A log entry with a stack trace becomes a single row containing the full multi-line text
First line of file always starts a new row regardless of format
Multiline joining is ON by default for .log extension
Unit test verifies: loading a file with ERROR + 3-line stack trace produces one row containing all 4 lines
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Pattern: line starts with '[' = new log entry. Anything else = continuation of previous entry. This matches opensearch.log Log4j2 format: [timestamp][level][component] [node] message.

userStories.title: CSV file support via Calcite CsvSchema
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to .load a CSV file so that it becomes a queryable table with columns inferred from the header row.
acceptanceCriteria: .load <path>.csv as <name> registers the CSV file as a Calcite table
Column names are inferred from the first (header) row
Column types are inferred from data sampling (string, integer, double)
Queries work: source = catalog.<name> | stats count() by <column>
A sample CSV file exists at cli/examples/orders.csv for testing
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use Calcite's built-in CsvSchema or implement a simple CSV parser that creates SimpleTable. Add calcite-csv dependency if using CsvSchema, or use Jackson CsvMapper.

userStories.title: Validate grok command through Unified Query API
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to use PPL grok command on loaded text data to extract structured fields from semi-structured log lines.
acceptanceCriteria: PPL grok command works end-to-end: source = catalog.logs | grok line pattern | where extracted_field = 'value'
Test with opensearch.log format: grok line '[%{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:ts}][%{LOGLEVEL:level}%{SPACE}][%{DATA:component}] [%{DATA:node}] %{GREEDYDATA:msg}'
Extracted fields (ts, level, component, node, msg) are usable in subsequent where/stats/sort commands
If grok does NOT work through Calcite-backed engine, document the specific gap in notes field
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: CRITICAL validation. If grok doesn't work through UnifiedQueryPlanner/Compiler, this is a blocker for the intern project. The grok patterns (TIMESTAMP_ISO8601, LOGLEVEL, DATA, GREEDYDATA) are defined in common/src/main/resources/patterns/patterns. GrokExpression is in core/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/expression/parse/GrokExpression.java.

userStories.title: Validate patterns command through Unified Query API
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to use PPL patterns command to automatically discover and group similar log messages.
acceptanceCriteria: PPL patterns command works: source = catalog.logs | grok line '...' | patterns msg method=simple_pattern
If method=brain is available, test that too
If patterns does NOT work through Calcite-backed engine, document the specific gap in notes field
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Lower priority than grok validation. PatternsExpression is in core/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/expression/parse/PatternsExpression.java.

userStories.title: Pre-built format support for opensearch-log
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want .load file --format opensearch-log to auto-parse OpenSearch application logs into structured columns (timestamp, level, component, node, message) without writing grok patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: .load opensearch.log as logs --format opensearch-log produces a table with columns: timestamp, level, component, node, message
The grok pattern is applied during loading, not at query time
Multiline joining is applied before grok parsing
Queries work directly on structured columns: source = catalog.logs | where level = 'ERROR' | stats count() by component
Format registry exists as a map of format name to grok pattern + config
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-010 (text loading), US-011 (multiline), and US-013 (grok validation). If grok doesn't work at query time, this becomes the alternative: parse during load into SimpleTable columns.

userStories.title: Context-aware tab completion from Calcite schema
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want tab completion to suggest table names after 'source =' and column names after 'where'/'stats'/'fields' based on the actual loaded schema.
acceptanceCriteria: After 'source =' or 'FROM ', tab suggests loaded table names
After '| where ', '| eval ', '| fields ', '| stats ... by ', tab suggests column names from the most recently referenced table
Column suggestions update when new data is loaded via .load
Existing keyword completion still works alongside schema completion
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Extend QueryCompleter to query UnifiedQueryContext catalog metadata for table/column names. Use JLine3 Completer interface with context-aware logic based on cursor position and preceding tokens.

userStories.title: Query timing and row count display
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to see execution time and row count after every query result.
acceptanceCriteria: Every query result shows 'N row(s) returned (Xms)' after the ASCII table
Timing measures the full plan() -> compile() -> executeQuery() -> format pipeline
Zero-row results show '0 row(s) returned (Xms)'
Non-interactive mode (-e) also shows timing
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Wrap query execution in System.nanoTime() timing. Update ResultSetFormatter to accept and display timing.

userStories.title: Inline bar chart display for numeric aggregations
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want inline ASCII bar charts next to numeric columns so I can visually see the distribution of values like error counts by component.
acceptanceCriteria: When query result has a numeric column, an optional bar column is appended showing proportional Unicode block bars
Bar width = (value / max_value) * max_bar_width using Unicode block chars
.chart on/off meta-command toggles bar chart display (default: on)
Works for stats count() by X, stats avg() by X, and similar aggregation results
Time-series detection: if group-by column looks like timestamp pattern, label bar column as 'trend'
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Extend ResultSetFormatter. Detect numeric columns from ResultSetMetaData. Use Unicode block elements: full block U+2588, left one-eighth block U+258F. Max bar width ~30 chars.

userStories.title: Machine-readable JSON output for agent cooperation
userStories.description: As an AI agent using the CLI as a tool, I want --output json to produce clean parseable JSON with no banners or noise, so I can programmatically query OpenSearch data.
acceptanceCriteria: --output json flag produces JSON array output for query results
Non-interactive mode (-e) + --output json: no ASCII banner, no 'Loaded' messages, ONLY JSON to stdout
.tables and .describe output structured JSON when --output json is active
Errors output as JSON: {error:true, type:string, message:string}
Exit codes: 0=success, 1=query error, 2=load/file error
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Critical for agent cooperation story. The CLI becomes an MCP tool server for AI agents. JSON output must be deterministic and parseable by json.loads().

userStories.title: Script mode for multi-command agent pipelines
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to send multiple commands (load + query) in one CLI invocation via --script or stdin so I can do multi-step investigation efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: --script flag or stdin pipe accepts multiple commands, one per line
Commands execute sequentially; each produces output
With --output json, each command's output is a separate JSON line (NDJSON)
.load commands produce status JSON; queries produce result arrays
Errors in one command don't abort the script (continue to next command)
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-019 (JSON output). Enables AI agents to do: load file -> inspect schema -> run query in one call.
